sonnet_review.biasedText: President Donald Trump thrust the United States into a war against Iran last weekend without bothering to secure congressional approval or even pretending to identify a legal basis for his actions.
sonnet_review.reason: This aligns with my detection of the same text as Spin bias. The phrases 'without bothering' and 'even pretending' are editorializing in the author's voice. The bias type differs slightly but the core detection is equivalent.

sonnet_review.biasedText: But this new conflict pushes Trump even deeper into dictatorial territory
sonnet_review.reason: Directly matches my detection of the same text as Word Choice bias. 'Dictatorial territory' is a loaded characterization in the author's voice.

sonnet_review.biasedText: This theory of executive supremacy leaves American armed forces at the whim of one man—a total inversion of our constitutional design.
sonnet_review.reason: Directly matches my detection of the same text and bias type. Strong interpretive conclusions presented as objective fact.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Trump's startling claim of unilateral war powers
sonnet_review.reason: Matches my detection of the same text as Spin. 'Startling' is a subjective editorializing adjective.
Matches my detection of the same text. The author presents this characterization in their own connecting prose without adequate distancing language.

gpt_review.biasedText: President Donald Trump thrust the United States into a war against Iran last weekend without bothering to secure congressional approval or even pretending to identify a legal basis for his actions.
gpt_review.reason: Directly matches my detection of the same text as Spin. The phrases 'without bothering' and 'even pretending' are loaded, editorializing language.

gpt_review.biasedText: This administration, of course, has already declared a freewheeling authority to commence hostilities in foreign nations without a green light from the legislative branch.
gpt_review.reason: Directly matches my detection of the same text and bias type. 'Freewheeling' is a subjective editorial judgment presented as fact.

gpt_review.biasedText: But this new conflict pushes Trump even deeper into dictatorial territory: He now asserts a freedom to disregard constitutional limits on his war-making authority and thrust the country into a potentially protracted military campaign that will only end on his say-so.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of 'dictatorial territory' as Word Choice bias, and the broader passage does present contested opinions as fact.

gpt_review.biasedText: This theory of executive supremacy leaves American armed forces at the whim of one man—a total inversion of our constitutional design.
gpt_review.reason: Directly matches my detection of the same text and bias type.

gpt_review.biasedText: Trump's startling claim of unilateral war powers.
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of the same text as Spin. 'Startling' is a subjective qualifier revealing the author's stance.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: This administration, of course, has already declared a freewheeling authority to commence hostilities in foreign nations without a green light from the legislative branch
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the characterization of authority as 'freewheeling,' which is a subjective editorial judgment presented as established fact in the author's voice.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Fidell—a visiting lecturer and senior research scholar at Yale Law School and expert on the law of armed conflict—has argued that the president's actions constitute not just a flagrant abuse of office but an impeachable offense
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed that the entire article features only one legal perspective (anti-Trump) with no opposing expert viewpoint presented, constituting bias by omission.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: this time, he is openly calling it a war, which it clearly is
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the author's editorial interjection 'which it clearly is,' presenting a contested legal characterization as self-evident.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: Fidell—a visiting lecturer and senior research scholar at Yale Law School and expert on the law of armed conflict—has argued that the president's actions constitute not just a flagrant abuse of office but an impeachable offense
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed that the article presents only one legal expert who holds an extreme anti-Trump position with no opposing perspective included anywhere.
GPT missed that 'flagrant' is a subjective intensifier presented in the author's own connecting prose without adequate distancing language.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: this time, he is openly calling it a war, which it clearly is
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed the author's editorial interjection 'which it clearly is,' which presents a contested legal characterization as self-evident fact.
